content.problemStatement:
You are given a string S0S_0S0​ of length NNN consisting of lowercase english alphabets.

We define the string Si=S(i−1)+S_i = S_{(i-1)} +Si​=S(i−1)​+ `reverse`(S(i−1))(S_{(i-1)})(S(i−1)​), where `reverse`(X)(X)(X) denotes the string obtained after reversing the string XXX.

Given an integer KKK, find the number of times S0S_0S0​ appears as a **substring** in SKS_KSK​.

Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK — the length of initial string and the number of operations.
The next line contains a string of length NNN consisting of lowercase english alphabets.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of times S0S_0S0​ appears as a **substring** in SKS_KSK​.
Since the answer can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: S0S_0S0​ consists of lowercase english alphabets only.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1062\cdot 10^62⋅106.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Given S0=S_0 =S0​= code.

S1=S_1=S1​= codeedoc.
S2=S_2=S2​= codeedoccodeedoc.

Total number of substrings in S2=S_2=S2​= codeedoccodeedoc that match S0=S_0=S0​= code is 222.
Test case 222: Given S0=S_0 =S0​= aa.

S1=S_1=S1​= aaaa.
S2=S_2=S2​= aaaaaaaa.
S3=S_3=S3​= aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa.

Total number of substrings in SK=S_K=SK​= aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa that match S0=S_0=S0​= aa is 151515.
Test case 333: Given S0=S_0=S0​= racecar. S3S_3S3​ will be racecarracecarracecarracecarracecarracecarracecarracecar.
Total number of substrings in SKS_KSK​ that match S0S_0S0​ is 888.
